error_signature: Client network socket disconnected before secure TLS connection was established
title: Gmail node SSL handshake failure on Docker Desktop
symptom: The Gmail node fails to connect, showing 'Client network socket disconnected before secure TLS connection was established'. This occurs during the SSL handshake process.
applies_when: Running n8n on Docker Desktop with WSL2
not_this_if: Running on a different OS or environment
root_cause: Docker on Windows with WSL2 can introduce network complexities such as MTU size issues, DNS resets, and IPv6 conflicts, which can lead to SSL handshake failures when connecting to external services like Gmail.
fix_steps: Reset the Windows network stack by running 'netsh winsock reset', 'netsh int ip reset', and 'ipconfig /flushdns' in PowerShell as Administrator, then restart your computer.
Fix WSL DNS by editing '/etc/wsl.conf' to prevent auto-generation and manually setting a reliable DNS in '/etc/resolv.conf'.
Update your 'docker-compose.yml' to set MTU to 1200 and ensure TLS 1.2 is used without quotes for NODE_OPTIONS.
Clean Docker networks by running 'docker compose down' and 'docker network prune', then restart the stack with 'docker compose up -d'.
agent_instruction: Follow the detailed steps to reset the network stack, fix WSL DNS, and update Docker configuration.
